revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:石楯尾神社]]


'''Iwatateo Shrine''' (石楯尾神社, Iwatateo-no-jinja, or Iwatateo-jinja) is one of the {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Sagami Province|ja|相模国の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Sagami Province|qq=}} (13 Shikinaisha in Sagami Province) listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, compiled in the 5th year of the {{ill|Enchō|en|Enchō|qid=Q1327274|lt=Engi (era)|1=Enchō}} era (927). Its location is described in the text as "Iwatateo Shrine in Takatsu District." 

In the past, the {{ill|Tsukui District, Kanagawa|en|Tsukui District, Kanagawa|qid=Q7850104|lt=Tsukui region|1=Tsukui District, Kanagawa}}, where the Iwatateo Shrine (Nagura Gongen) is currently located, was part of Aikō District and referred to as "Oku-Miho." This region encompassed the left bank of the Sagami River, which included parts of present-day {{ill|Sagamihara|en|Sagamihara|qid=Q209779|lt=Sagamihara|1=Sagamihara}}, such as the northern areas of former {{ill|Tsukui, Kanagawa|en|Tsukui, Kanagawa|qid=Q7850102|lt=Tsukui Town|1=Tsukui, Kanagawa}}, {{ill|Shiroyama Town|ja|城山町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiroyama Town|1=shiroyamamachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11426118}}, {{ill|Fujino, Kanagawa|en|Fujino, Kanagawa|qid=Q5507456|lt=Fujino Town|1=Fujino, Kanagawa}}, and {{ill|Sagamiko, Kanagawa|en|Sagamiko, Kanagawa|qid=Q7398965|lt=Lake Sagami Town|1=Sagamiko, Kanagawa}}. These areas were originally part of Takatsu District.<ref>[[津久井郡#歴史]]</ref><ref>[[相模原市#歴史]]</ref> However, ancient documents about the shrine were lost in a fire during the Muromachi period. 

Consequently, among the shrines bearing the same name, Iwatateo Shrine in Takatsu District remains the only contender for the original designation. Today, there are seven {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=shrines|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kanagawa Prefecture considered candidates for the title of Iwatateo Shrine. This article will cover all these shrines.

== Iwatateo Shrine (Nagura Gongen) ==
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]
| name      = Iwatateo Shrine (Nagura Gongen)
| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]
|image     = File:Isobe-Iwadateo jinja.JPG
| location  = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|qid=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}, {{ill|Sagamihara|en|Sagamihara|qid=Q209779|lt=Sagamihara|1=Sagamihara}}, {{ill|Midori-ku, Sagamihara|en|Midori-ku, Sagamihara|qid=Q1132502|lt=Midori Ward (Sagamihara)|1=Midori-ku, Sagamihara}}, {{ill|Nagura (Sagamihara)|ja|名倉 (相模原市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagura (Sagamihara)|1=Nagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11414729}} 4524
| coordinates = {{Coord|35.614333|N|139.131833|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
| deity     = Iwatateo Ōkami
| Classification = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor), {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} (former Ranking)
| Founding  = Unknown
| Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=Shinmei-zukuri}}
| Alternative name = Nagura Gongen
| festival = August 26
| location = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|qid=Q127513|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}
| architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}}
}}

'''Iwatateo Shrine''' (石楯尾神社, Iwatateo-no-jinja), located in {{ill|Nagura|ja|名倉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagura|1=nagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11414727}}, {{ill|Midori-ku|en|Midori-ku|qid=Q1140937|lt=Midori Ward (Sagamihara)|1=Midori-ku}}, Sagamihara City, Kanagawa Prefecture, is a shrine also known as "Nagura no Gongen-sama." Its precincts feature a pair of sacred cedar trees (夫婦杉), estimated to be 400 years old, with a height of 40 meters and a trunk circumference of 3.7 meters. These trees, along with the forest surrounding the shrine, are designated as Kanagawa Prefecture Natural Monuments.<ref name="fujino1">{{Cite web|url=https://info-fujino.com/art/6761.html|title=【History】Iwatateo Shrine|publisher=Fujino Tourism Association|accessdate=2023-06-08}}</ref>

According to tradition, the shrine was originally located near the border with {{ill|Kai Province|en|Kai Province|qid=Q858076|lt=Kai Province|1=Kai Province}} (modern Yamanashi Prefecture). It was later moved to its current location in the Takatsu District (modern Sagamihara area). The ancient records suggest that the shrine's original location marked the northwestern boundary of the district, which extended along the Sagami River. 

=== Worship of the Eboshi Rock ===
In earlier times, worship focused on the "Eboshi Rock," a sacred stone situated near the eastern side of the shrine. However, this stone was removed during the construction of the {{ill|Chūō Main Line|en|Chūō Main Line|qid=Q1078110|lt=Chūō Main Line|1=Chūō Main Line}} in the Meiji era. The shrine’s name, "Iwatateo," is believed to reference this rock, as it was thought to represent a "tail" of the rocky ridge.

=== Historical Significance ===
According to legend, Emperor Ōjin visited the shrine, where it underwent renovations. Records from the {{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|ja|文徳実録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本文徳天皇実録}} mention the shrine in the 1st year of the {{ill|Ten'an|en|Ten'an|qid=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an era|1=Ten'an}} (857) as a government-supported shrine. The shrine was listed as a minor shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. During the {{ill|Battle of Mimasetoge|en|Battle of Mimasetoge|qid=Q1133861|lt=Battle of Mimase Pass|1=Battle of Mimasetoge}} in {{ill|Eiroku 12 (1569)|ja|永禄12年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiroku 12 (1569)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579189|1=1569}}, the shrine was destroyed by fire, and its ancient records were lost.

The current main hall was constructed in {{ill|Kyōhō 9 (1724)|ja|享保9年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōhō 9 (1724)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579190|1=1724}}. The shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} in 1873 under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qid=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} system. In 1923, it was designated as a shrine eligible to receive offerings from the national government. In 1948, it became a shrine under the Kanagawa Shrine Association.

=== Deities ===
* Iwatateo Ōkami
* {{ill|Ubusunagami|en|Ubusunagami|qid=Q1392097|lt=Ubusunagami|1=Ubusunagami}}
* {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}}
* {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主神|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}}
* {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高御産巣日神|1=Takamimusubi|qid=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}
* {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}
* {{ill|Toshigami|lt=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-Kami|ja|大歳神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi-no-Kami|qq=}}
* {{ill|Saratobi-no-Kami|ja|猿田彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saratobi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3090037}}
* {{ill|Kikuri-hime|ja|菊理姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuri-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 菊理媛神|1=Kikurihime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11619891}}
* {{ill|Kotoamatsukami|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoamatsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}}
* {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q461258}}
* {{ill|Prince Moriyoshi|en|Prince Moriyoshi|qid=Q2288858|lt=Prince Moriyoshi|1=Prince Moriyoshi}}

=== Festivals ===
* February - {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 節分|1=Setsubun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q746798}}
* April 10 - {{ill|Spring Festival|ja|春季大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579192|1=Spring Matsuri}}
* August 26 - {{ill|Annual Grand Festival|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Annual Grand Festival|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}}
* November 28 - {{ill|Autumn Festival|ja|秋季大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579193|1=Autumn Matsuri}}
* First day of each month - Monthly Festival
* Full Moon - Spiritual Unveiling Ceremony (registration required at the shrine office)

=== Transportation ===
* From {{ill|Fujino Station|en|Fujino Station|qid=Q5507458|lt=Fujino Station|1=Fujino Station}}, take the {{ill|Fujikyu Bus|ja|富士急バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujikyu Bus|1=Fujikyu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11456051}} "Nagura Circulation Line" to "Nagura," then walk 13 minutes.
* Alternatively, Fujino Station is a 30-minute walk away.
